reasoning: The Polymarket base rate of 0.42 likely applies to the positive phrasing "Will Trump announce a next Fed Chair in 2025?", which means the probability he REFRAINS (the specific question here) was about 0.58. The evidence strongly supports a delay into 2026: (1) The timeline for interviews and announcements has consistently slipped since June 2025. (2) Treasury Sec Bessent stated he will present the final slate of 3-4 candidates to Trump "sometime after Thanksgiving" (Nov 27), leaving a very short holiday-interrupted window in December for interviews, vetting, and an announcement. (3) Trump's phrasing on Oct 27 was non-committal, stating a decision "might be" made by year-end. (4) The original "shadow chair" idea that prompted rumors of an early 2025 announcement has reportedly been walked back by Bessent himself. Given typical government and administrative delays—coupled with the Christmas/New Year holidays—there is a greater than 50% chance the announcement slips into early 2026 (Jan/Feb being the historically normal time to announce a replacement for a term expiring in May). I predict a 56% probability that he refrains from making the announcement in 2025.
